message:
beads: file dhjov (fcp-registry SupplyChainVerificationConfig orphan, P1/HIGH)

Audit finding: crates/fcp-registry/src/lib.rs:513-528 defines a public
SupplyChainVerificationConfig struct with six fields (tuf_pinned_root,
trusted_sigstore_identities, trusted_sigstore_issuers,
require_transparency, require_tuf, require_sigstore) — all six are
orphan. Whole-crate grep confirms the fields are only read by tests;
no production path threads the config into RegistryVerifier::verify_bundle
or any downstream check.

The only production "require_transparency"-shaped gate is at lib.rs:1517
reading `policy.require_transparency_log` — that is SupplyChainPolicy
(manifest-embedded), a different struct. An operator who believes
setting SupplyChainVerificationConfig { require_tuf: true, .. } enables
TUF-required installs gets silent no-op.

Full analysis, fix options (remove / thread through verify_bundle /
move into RegistryVerifier), and verification plan are in the bead body.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

## Must validate

- default deny (no CapabilityToken → deny + DecisionReceipt)
- NetworkConstraints enforcement
- receipts/audit for Dangerous operations
- structured logs

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E run is deterministic and produces a stable artifact bundle.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ddc","title":"fcp.terraform: Module management and dependency resolution","description":"# fcp.terraform: Module management and dependency resolution (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide safe visibility into Terraform module structure so agents can:
- inspect required providers/modules
- analyze dependency graph
- prepare plans with correct initialization

## Scope
- Run `terraform init` in a controlled, non-interactive mode (may download modules/providers).
- List required providers and versions.
- Enumerate modules and sources.
- Produce a canonical `ModuleGraph` summary.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `terraform.modules.read`
- `terraform.init` (dangerous-ish if it downloads content; policy-gated)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- Sandbox profile:
  - restrict filesystem to workspace
  - restrict process execution
- NetworkConstraints default deny unless module registries are explicitly allowed.
- No automatic downloads unless policy explicitly permits.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `terraform.providers.list`
  - `terraform.modules.graph`
  - Optional: `terraform.init`
- Canonical output type:
  - `ModuleGraph`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Unit tests with fixtures for:
  - module parsing
  - provider list parsing
  - policy denial of init without explicit capability

## Acceptance criteria
- Module graph summaries are deterministic and safe-by-default.

InitPolicy for download control. 129 tests, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-03-09T19:05:54Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-dgi","title":"fcp.mcp-bridge: Model Context Protocol Gateway Connector","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:27:18.399804049Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T17:08:22.932008209Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T17:08:22.932008209Z","close_reason":"DUPLICATE: Merged with flywheel_connectors-7ju which has the complete MCP Gateway Meta-Connector specification.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-dgi","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-iv7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-dgi","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-krt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-dgi","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-zx0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-dhjov","title":"fcp-registry: SupplyChainVerificationConfig fields (require_tuf, require_sigstore, require_transparency, tuf_pinned_root, trusted_sigstore_identities, trusted_sigstore_issuers) are orphan — never consulted in verify path","description":"## Finding

`crates/fcp-registry/src/lib.rs:513-528` defines a public `SupplyChainVerificationConfig` struct with six configuration fields (`tuf_pinned_root`, `trusted_sigstore_identities`, `trusted_sigstore_issuers`, `require_transparency`, `require_tuf`, `require_sigstore`). None of these fields are consumed in a gating check anywhere in the production verification path.

Grep evidence (whole-workspace, excluding tests):
- `grep -rn '\.require_tuf\|\.require_sigstore' crates/` — matches only test assertions and field initializers, never a gating `if`.
- `grep -rn 'SupplyChainVerificationConfig' crates/fcp-registry/src/` — type is defined at lib.rs:515, never used as a parameter to any verify function.
- `RegistryVerifier::verify_bundle` at lib.rs:872 takes only `bundle`, `zone_policy`, `supply_chain`, `expected_target`. There is no path for operator-configured `require_tuf`/`require_sigstore` to flow into verification.

The single production consumer of a "require_transparency"-shaped boolean is `enforce_supply_chain_policy` at lib.rs:1517 (`if policy.require_transparency_log { ... }`) — but that is **`SupplyChainPolicy`** (the manifest-embedded policy), not **`SupplyChainVerificationConfig`**. They are two different structs; operators who set `SupplyChainVerificationConfig.require_transparency = true` do not get what they expect.

## Why this is P1

**Policy-bypass-by-default.** An operator who wants to enforce "this host only installs connectors with valid TUF metadata" today reaches for `SupplyChainVerificationConfig { require_tuf: true, tuf_pinned_root: Some(root), .. }`. That configuration is silently inert. The operator believes they have enabled TUF enforcement; the verifier accepts bundles with no TUF check at all.

Same logic for `require_sigstore` and `require_transparency`.

**Compounds the `MockTufVerifier` / `MockSigstoreVerifier` footgun (br-grudr, now closed).** Even with mocks correctly feature-gated, a downstream deployment that wires up real TUF/Sigstore verifiers but never consults their output is indistinguishable, at the verifier boundary, from a deployment that skipped the wiring entirely.

## Fix (sketch)

Three options, in order of increasing invasiveness:

1. **Remove the dead fields.** If the design intent never was for `SupplyChainVerificationConfig` to gate verification, delete the struct (or at minimum strip `require_*`/`tuf_pinned_root`) and make operators rely on `SupplyChainPolicy.require_transparency_log` + the per-verifier implementations.

2. **Thread the config through `RegistryVerifier::verify_bundle`.** Add a `supply_chain_config: Option<&SupplyChainVerificationConfig>` parameter and implement the gates:
   - If `require_tuf` and no TUF evidence passed verification → `RegistryError::TufVerificationRequired`.
   - If `require_sigstore` and no Sigstore evidence passed → `RegistryError::SigstoreVerificationRequired`.
   - If `require_transparency` and no Rekor entry was verified → `RegistryError::TransparencyEvidenceMissing`.
   - If `tuf_pinned_root` is Some, pass it into the `TufVerifier`.

3. **Move responsibility onto `RegistryVerifier`.** Store the config as a field in `RegistryVerifier` (alongside `trust_policy`) and consult it in `verify_bundle` before returning `Ok`.

Verification after fix: add a production-path test that constructs a `RegistryVerifier` with `require_tuf: true`, no TUF evidence, and asserts the verifier rejects the bundle. The current test suite (lib.rs:4906+, 6927+, 9465+) only asserts the struct round-trips its fields.

## Severity

P1 / HIGH. The struct's existence creates a false sense of security — setting any of its flags gives no enforcement. Operators assuming they have TUF-required installs do not.

## Related

- br-grudr (CLOSED): Mock*Verifier feature-gate. This bead is the other half of that finding — even with mocks correctly gated, the verify path itself has no config-driven require-policy enforcement.
- br-2ot4f (security audit): this is a new finding that the earlier audit missed; filed under the same threat model.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:23:23.929978Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:23:23.929978Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-03-05T22:14:30Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-dij3a","title":"Implement firecrawl connector: web scraping/crawling via Firecrawl API","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:13:54.009411Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:50:02.650323Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:50:02.650143Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","hardening","implementation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-dij3a","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1i9ky","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:14:05.160725Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1962,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-dij3a","author":"jemanuel","text":"Firecrawl (https://firecrawl.dev) is a web scraping API. Well-documented REST API at https://docs.firecrawl.dev/api-reference.

Key operations to implement:
- scrape_url: Scrape a single URL and return markdown/HTML
- crawl_url: Crawl a website starting from a URL
- map_url: Get a sitemap of a website
- search: Search the web and return scraped results

The scaffold already declares these operations. Implementation requires:
1. Build real HTTP client with Firecrawl API key auth
2. Implement request/response types for each operation
3. Add ConnectorErrorMapping for Firecrawl-specific errors
4. Add rate limiting per their docs (varies by plan)
5. Remove invoke_surface_unimplemented gate
6. Add wiremock tests for each operation
7. Update manifest.toml: change status from stub to ready","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:14:04Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-djx","title":"fcp.airtable: Base and table discovery API","description":"# fcp.airtable: Base and Table Discovery API

## Goal
Implement Airtable discovery operations so agents can understand what data exists before attempting record CRUD.

## External surface (NetworkConstraints)
Default deny.
- Network egress MUST be restricted per-operation via manifest `NetworkConstraints`.
- Allow only the configured service host(s); HTTPS-only where applicable; deny redirects by default.
- Bound timeouts + response sizes; treat all remote responses as untrusted input; never leak secrets/PII in logs or errors.

## Zone model (mechanical)

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

Recommended deployment pattern: run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies.

## Scope
Operations (representative):
- `airtable.bases.list`
- `airtable.bases.get` (schema)
- `airtable.tables.list`
- `airtable.tables.get`
- `airtable.fields.list`

## Key requirements
- Capability gated (`airtable.schema.read`).
- Prefer stable identifiers:
  - support referencing fields by **field id** (stable across renames) and name (human-readable).
- Cache schema responses with bounded TTL to reduce API load.
- Strict input validation:
  - base/table identifiers canonicalized
  - reject ambiguous references

## Tests
- Mock-only API responses for:
  - schema changes (rename fields, change options)
  - permission failures
  - rate limits
- Logs are structured JSON (redacted).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Schema discovery is deterministic and stable across renames.
- Conformance/E2E runner can validate default deny + allow paths.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"OrangeRidge","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:14:32.913194446Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-03T06:00:38.854424Z","closed_at":"2026-03-03T06:00:38.854401Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","fcp2","tier2"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-djx","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-soft","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-djx","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-soft.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -1246,7 +1247,7 @@
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-g37n0","title":"fcp-slack: ok=true responses can panic on missing payload data","description":"## Finding
`SlackApiResponse<T>` stores the flattened success payload as `Option<T>` ([connectors/slack/src/types.rs:123-129](connectors/slack/src/types.rs:123)). `check_response()` only checks `ok` ([connectors/slack/src/client.rs:447-458](connectors/slack/src/client.rs:447)). Multiple client paths then call `resp.data.expect("ok response has data")` ([connectors/slack/src/client.rs:162](connectors/slack/src/client.rs:162), [209](connectors/slack/src/client.rs:209), [228](connectors/slack/src/client.rs:228), [238](connectors/slack/src/client.rs:238), [257](connectors/slack/src/client.rs:257), [270](connectors/slack/src/client.rs:270), [282](connectors/slack/src/client.rs:282), [302](connectors/slack/src/client.rs:302), [312](connectors/slack/src/client.rs:312)).

A malformed or partial `ok:true` body therefore becomes a process panic instead of an `FcpError`/`SlackError`. The crate already documents the `None` case in tests (`slack_api_response_error_has_no_data`), so this is not theoretical parser behavior.

## Repro shape
Return `{"ok":true}` or `{"ok":true,"channel":"C1"}` from `chat.postMessage`. `check_response()` accepts it and the subsequent `.expect(...)` aborts the connector.

## Recommendation
Replace each `.expect("ok response has data")` with an error-mapped `ok_or_else(...)` path that preserves the operation context and treats malformed success envelopes as API/protocol errors, not panics.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:56:43.789357Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T14:23:29.875248Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T14:23:29.872787Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2811,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-g37n0","author":"jemanuel","text":"Fixed in 2dd92d08. Root cause: 9 client paths called .expect("ok response has data") on a flattened Option<T> payload — a partial {"ok":true} envelope therefore panicked the connector instead of surfacing as an error. Fix: added Self::expect_data(data, method) helper that maps None to a terminal SlackError::Api { ok: true, error: "Slack API method \`X\` returned ok=true with no payload", .. }, and migrated all 9 call sites through it. The ok:true discriminator preserves the partial-envelope case as distinct from classic ok=false errors. Regression test ok_true_with_missing_payload_is_mapped_to_api_error_not_panic asserts the method name and 'ok=true' phrase appear in the error, and that to_fcp_error() maps to FcpError::External. Verified: 210 lib tests + 51/52 integration tests pass (the one pre-existing failure list_channels_happy_path is an unrelated URL-encoding bug confirmed by bisect).","created_at":"2026-04-21T14:23:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-gg5sl","title":"[FCP3/P3.2] Define canonical FCPS durable object schemas for runtime truth","description":"Background: a mesh first platform needs durable forms for the facts that survive process death and justify later decisions. Receipts, checkpoints, lease state, revocations, connector runtime state, and operator explanations cannot remain host local conventions.

Scope: define canonical FCPS object schemas for durable state, including connector identity and state, receipts, checkpoints, lease and fencing records, revocation records, and explanation bundles. Design them for replay, repair, inspection, and failover rather than as opaque storage details. Provide deterministic object examples, snapshot formats, explanation bundle shapes, schema unit tests, replay integration tests, and detailed inspection logs that later conformance and end to end harnesses can diff and diagnose.

Acceptance: the platform has a durable object vocabulary for the facts it must remember, those facts no longer depend on one host memory image to exist, and the schemas are concrete enough for fixture driven validation, replay, operator inspection, and regression tests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:05.315492Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-03-28T06:31:09.065699Z","closed_at":"2026-03-28T06:31:09.065467Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["durability","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","fcps","mesh-first","phase-3"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-gg5sl","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-2m2hl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:24.219178Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-gg5sl","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-d8jbq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:24.654535Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2081,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-gg5sl","author":"jemanuel","text":"WhiteCompass completed 2026-03-28: Published FCPS durable object schemas at docs/FCP3_FCPS_Durable_Object_Schemas.md. Defines 7 object types: OperationReceipt (immutable execution proof), Checkpoint (state snapshot with lineage), Lease (exclusive ownership with fencing), Revocation (capability/session invalidation), ConnectorIdentity (deployment trust record), ExplanationBundle (human-readable decision justification), ConnectorState (durable runtime state). Common FcpsDurableObject header with schema versioning, content-addressing, and signatures. Includes fixtures, evolution rules, and inspection CLI commands.","created_at":"2026-03-28T06:31:08Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-gh0j7","title":"fuzz/fcps_frame: target only decodes frames/symbols without checking accepted-state invariants","description":"File: fuzz/fuzz_targets/fcps_frame.rs:18-37

Finding: the fuzz body only calls `FcpsFrameHeader::decode`, `FcpsFrame::decode`, `SymbolRecord::decode`, and `FrameFlags::from_bits_truncate`, then drops all successful results.

Why this matters: the target is good for panic discovery but weak for semantic coverage. It never checks that successfully decoded frames and symbol records obey internal size/header invariants.

Suggested follow-up: when decode succeeds, assert header/frame consistency, record bounds consistency, and stable behavior across repeated decode paths.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:20:10.749754Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:20:10.749754Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-gh7gt","title":"fcp-raptorq: encode_all/into_encode_all ESI arithmetic uses wrapping add, not checked_add","description":"In `crates/fcp-raptorq/src/encode.rs:141-144` (`encode_all`) and `crates/fcp-raptorq/src/encode.rs:162-166` (`into_encode_all`), the repair symbol ESI is computed as `k_prime + i` where both are u32. In debug builds, overflow panics; in release, it wraps into the source-symbol ESI range and silently collides with existing source symbols.

**Bounded in practice.** `config.repair_symbols(source_symbols)` saturates to `u32::MAX`, but `source_symbols` comes from `payload_len.div_ceil(symbol_size)` where `payload_len ≤ config.max_object_size` (typically 64 MiB) and `symbol_size >= 1`. So `source_symbols ≤ 64 MiB`, well under u32::MAX. The current config bounds keep the arithmetic safe.

**Brittle.** The rest of the crate uses `saturating_add` / `checked_add` consistently (see `config.rs:155`, `decode.rs:585`, `decode.rs:598`). `encode.rs:142` and `encode.rs:164` are the only sites that use raw `+` on user-derived-ish arithmetic with no bound check.

**Suggested fix**: `let esi = k_prime.checked_add(i).ok_or(EncodeError::SymbolCountOverflow)?;` (or saturating_add). Mirror the `decode.rs:585` pattern.

Severity: LOW. Not currently exploitable; filed as a defensive-depth bead to keep the saturating/checked arithmetic discipline uniform across the crate.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:14:51.293708Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:14:51.293708Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-gh7gt","title":"fcp-raptorq: encode_all/into_encode_all ESI arithmetic uses wrapping add, not checked_add","description":"In `crates/fcp-raptorq/src/encode.rs:141-144` (`encode_all`) and `crates/fcp-raptorq/src/encode.rs:162-166` (`into_encode_all`), the repair symbol ESI is computed as `k_prime + i` where both are u32. In debug builds, overflow panics; in release, it wraps into the source-symbol ESI range and silently collides with existing source symbols.

**Bounded in practice.** `config.repair_symbols(source_symbols)` saturates to `u32::MAX`, but `source_symbols` comes from `payload_len.div_ceil(symbol_size)` where `payload_len ≤ config.max_object_size` (typically 64 MiB) and `symbol_size >= 1`. So `source_symbols ≤ 64 MiB`, well under u32::MAX. The current config bounds keep the arithmetic safe.

**Brittle.** The rest of the crate uses `saturating_add` / `checked_add` consistently (see `config.rs:155`, `decode.rs:585`, `decode.rs:598`). `encode.rs:142` and `encode.rs:164` are the only sites that use raw `+` on user-derived-ish arithmetic with no bound check.

**Suggested fix**: `let esi = k_prime.checked_add(i).ok_or(EncodeError::SymbolCountOverflow)?;` (or saturating_add). Mirror the `decode.rs:585` pattern.

Severity: LOW. Not currently exploitable; filed as a defensive-depth bead to keep the saturating/checked arithmetic discipline uniform across the crate.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:14:51.293708Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:23:43.532Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:23:43.531732Z","close_reason":"Fix landed in c3cc8695 (saturate repair_count against k_prime overflow)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-gif","title":"fcp.pinecone: Pinecone Vector Database Connector","description":"# fcp.pinecone - Pinecone Vector Database Connector (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide an FCP2-compliant connector for Pinecone (managed vector DB) supporting vector storage and semantic retrieval under strict zone isolation.

## Manifest archetypes (closed set)
- `storage`
- `knowledge`

## Zone model
Single-zone bound: one connector instance ↔ one `ZoneId`.

## External surface
### NetworkConstraints
Default-deny; allow only the configured Pinecone endpoint(s) and port(s) required by declared operations.

### Credential injection
- API key via `CredentialId`.
- Never persist or log keys.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `pinecone.index.manage`
- `pinecone.vectors.read`
- `pinecone.vectors.write`
- `pinecone.search.query`

## Operations (initial)
- index: create/describe/list/delete
- vectors: upsert/fetch/delete/query

## Tests
- Mock-only unit tests with wiremock.
- E2E compliance run validates default deny + NetworkConstraints + receipts for writes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Passes connector compliance runner.
- Deterministic tests (no live Pinecone calls).

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-gif.1","title":"fcp.pinecone: Manifest + Capability Map (FCP2)","description":"# fcp.pinecone: Manifest + Capability Map (FCP2)

## Goal
Define the Pinecone connector manifest contract:
- archetypes (`storage`, `knowledge`)
- operations + schema IDs
- capability families + per-operation caps
- NetworkConstraints allowlist bound to configured endpoint

## Acceptance Criteria
- Complete manifest spec stored here.
- Good/bad manifest examples exist for static compliance.
